role: A React region component that renders the 'while you were away' morning greeting pill and its overlay card, fetching aggregated arrival context from /api/greeting.
capabilities: Fetch /api/greeting with optional ?since from localStorage last-visit timestamp
Track last visit in localStorage and auto-open the card after AWAY_MS (4h) when there is content
Render a persistent '● while you were away' pill button
Render an overlay HUD card with current mode/dials, returned memories, built headlines, and waiting-on-you items
Toggle expansion of the built list beyond six items
Open the card on a custom window 'open-greeting' event
substance_why: The file implements a complete, wired feature: data fetching, visit tracking, auto-open logic, event handling, and a multi-section rendered card.
interactions_present: Click pill to toggle card open/closed
interactions_missing: No loading or error UI for the greeting fetch
No retry or refresh after initial mount
No keyboard/escape-to-close or focus trap for the overlay
No explicit accessibility roles/aria for the overlay or pill
No interaction on waiting-on-you items (they are read-only text)
No way to dismiss or act on returned memories
performance_risks: Synchronous localStorage read/write on the main thread during effect init
Unconditional fetch on every mount with no request caching or deduplication beyond module-level bootedThisLoad guard
No memoization of derived lists or event handlers, causing re-renders of the card body on trivial state changes
Module-level bootedThisLoad flag is a fragile StrictMode workaround and breaks component-level test isolation
reuse_verdict_why: The greeting concept and data contract are valuable, but the presentation, state management, and lifecycle logic should be rebuilt into separated, testable pieces.
decomposition_hints: Split visit-tracking and /api/greeting fetch into a custom hook or service (e.g., useGreeting)
Split the pill trigger into its own small chrome component
Split the overlay card into a generic HUD/Dialog shell plus section subcomponents: ReturnedMemories, BuiltList, WaitingList
Separate the auto-open decision policy from rendering so it can be tested independently
